data.title: PayPoint unveils shake-up, confirms revenues in line with guidance
data.description:
The British firm currently owns a number of brands, including Collect+, Handepay and Park Christmas Savings, spread across four divisions: shopping, e-commerce, payments and banking, and Love2Shop

However, updating on year-end trading, and Paypoint confirmed it intended reorganise into four dedicated business units: network services, digital payments and open banking, Love2Shop and merchant services.

It argued: "This will result in a better integrated and more transparent business with a simpler investment case.

"The reorganisation will establish four business units of scale, with clearly defined operating structures, a greater focus on growth opportunities with a more accountable operating culture."

Each unit will have "clearly defined" financial metrics and key performance indicators, it added. The largest division is network services, with estimated net revenues of £91.3m in the year to 31 March. Revenues were forecast to reach £53.2m at Love2Shop, £31.5m at merchant services and £13.4m at digital payments and open banking.

Overall group revenues were "broadly in line" with expectations, PayPoint noted.

Looking to the current year, it continued: "In a challenging trading environment, our business reorganisation will ensure there are a number of important changes in priority and emphasis across the business.

"In terms of trading, outlook for the year ahead is balanced between the continued growth across the group, further cost efficiency initiatives and recent trends in certain of our business units, in particular our parcels business.

"Overall this indicates the business is likely to exceed the underlying profits delivered in the 2026 full year and within the range of market expectations."

PayPoint is scheduled to publish full-year numbers on 11 June.

As at 1000 BST, the FTSE All-Share stock had put on 2% at 570.8p.

See latest RNS on Investegate

data.title: UK: TotalEnergies Completes the Merger of its UK North Sea Upstream Assets with NEO NEXT
data.description:
PARIS--(BUSINESS WIRE)--March 30, 2026--

TotalEnergies announces the completion of the merger between NEO NEXT and TotalEnergies' UK Upstream Oil & Gas business. The combined group is renamed NEO NEXT+ and TotalEnergies holds a 47.5% shareholding interest in it.

Further to this transaction, NEO NEXT+ becomes the largest independent Oil and Gas producer on the UK Continental Shelf with an expected 2026 production of over 250,000 barrels of oil equivalent per day.

"The completion of this merger and the creation of NEO NEXT+ marks an important step in TotalEnergies' long--term commitment to the UK Oil and Gas sector. While contributing to the country's energy supply, the size and asset portfolio of NEO NEXT+ will foster synergies and enhance the cash flow generation of the company", said Patrick Pouyanné, Chairman and CEO of TotalEnergies. "As the new largest shareholder of NEO NEXT+, we are pleased to bring our extensive UK North Sea operational experience to the new company."

About TotalEnergies in the UK

TotalEnergies has been present in the UK for more than 60 years, employing more than 1,800 people across the energy value chain.

The Company operated around 27% of the UK Continental Shelf's gas production in 2025, with average daily equity production of 104,500 barrels of oil equivalent per day (boe/d).

TotalEnergies is deploying its Integrated Power strategy in the UK, which combines renewable power production and flexible power generation capacities. Its renewable portfolio in the country includes 1.1 GW of gross installed capacity (Seagreen offshore wind farm) and 5 GW under development, in both offshore wind and solar projects. TotalEnergies also holds a 50% stake in a 1.3 GW combined cycle gas turbine (CCGT) operated in partnership with EPUKI.

TotalEnergies is one of the UK's largest suppliers of gas and electricity to businesses and the public sector, offers electric vehicle charging solutions and markets petroleum products including lubricants, aviation fuel, bitumen and specialty fluids.

About TotalEnergies

TotalEnergies is a global integrated energy company that produces and markets energies: oil and biofuels, natural gas, biogas and low-carbon hydrogen, renewables and electricity. Our more than 100,000 employees are committed to providing as many people as possible with energy that is more reliable, more affordable and more sustainable. Active in about 120 countries, TotalEnergies places sustainability at the heart of its strategy, its projects and its operations.

@TotalEnergies TotalEnergies TotalEnergies TotalEnergies

Cautionary Note

The terms "TotalEnergies", "TotalEnergies company" or "Company" in this document are used to designate TotalEnergies SE and the consolidated entities that are directly or indirectly controlled by TotalEnergies SE. Likewise, the words "we", "us" and "our" may also be used to refer to these entities or to their employees. The entities in which TotalEnergies SE directly or indirectly owns a shareholding are separate legal entities. TotalEnergies SE has no liability for the acts or omissions of these entities. This document may contain forward-looking information and statements that are based on a number of economic data and assumptions made in a given economic, competitive and regulatory environment. They may prove to be inaccurate in the future and are subject to a number of risk factors. Neither TotalEnergies SE nor any of its subsidiaries assumes any obligation to update publicly any forward-looking information or statement, objectives or trends contained in this document whether as a result of new information, future events or otherwise. Information concerning risk factors, that may affect TotalEnergies' financial results or activities is provided in the most recent Registration Document, the French-language version of which is filed by TotalEnergies SE with the French securities regulator Autorité des Marchés Financiers (AMF), and in the Form 20-F filed with the United States Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC).

data.title: ZAWYA-PRESSR: Al Ain Region to elevate tourism offering with strategic hotel renovation and investment initiative
data.description:
​​​​​Abu Dhabi, UAE: As part of ongoing efforts to position Al Ain Region as a key tourist destination, and in alignment with strategic tourism development projects in the area, a Hotel Refurbishment Scheme has been launched to enhance the quality of hotel accommodations in Al Ain Region, elevating the overall visitor experience.

Implemented by the Department of Culture and Tourism – Abu Dhabi (DCT Abu Dhabi), this initiative proactively addresses hotel development needs to ensure the hospitality infrastructure continues to meet and surpass global standards. Under the scheme, hotel owners with properties that are eligible can receive a capital expenditure rebate of up to 12% of eligible investments. An additional 5% premium rebate is available for upgrades from unbranded to branded hotels, increases in star rating, and renovations of heritage properties, varying by star rating. The rebates will be disbursed after the renovation has been completed.

The initiative arrives at a time of strong growth for Abu Dhabi’s tourism sector. Al Ain Region welcomed 473,077 guests in 2025 (+9% year-on-year). Revenue per available room (RevPAR) reached AED 204 (+17% year-on-year), while occupancy was at 66% (+9% year-on-year). These positive trends underscore the importance of ongoing investment in high-quality infrastructure to sustain and accelerate growth.

H.E. Saood Abdulaziz Al Hosani, Undersecretary of DCT Abu Dhabi, said: “Al Ain Region is a living testament to the legacy of the UAE’s Founding Father, the late H.H. Sheikh Zayed bin Sultan Al Nahyan. It is a cornerstone of Abu Dhabi’s cultural identity and a vital pillar of our tourism ecosystem. The Hotel Refurbishment Scheme reflects our commitment to preserving the unique charm of this region, while elevating hospitality standards to ensure its offerings are world-class. By cultivating a vibrant and competitive tourism environment, this initiative will attract a wider range of visitors seeking adventure, wellness, and authentic cultural experiences, all enhanced with modern comforts.”

Al Ain Region, renowned as Abu Dhabi’s “living oasis” and home to The Cultural Sites of Al Ain, a UNESCO World Heritage Site, boasts a rich history, with evidence of human presence spanning 8,000 years. Its unique blend of cultural heritage, world-class hospitality, and adventure tourism has earned it the prestigious title of Gulf Tourism Capital for 2025 and Arab Capital of Tourism for 2026.

Strategic investments like the Hotel Refurbishment Scheme will further elevate Al Ain Region’s appeal as a destination where deep-rooted heritage is seamlessly complemented by an enhanced hospitality sector.

About the Department of Culture and Tourism – Abu Dhabi:

The Department of Culture and Tourism – Abu Dhabi (DCT Abu Dhabi) drives the sustainable growth of Abu Dhabi’s culture and tourism sectors, fuels economic progress and helps achieve Abu Dhabi’s wider global ambitions. By working in partnership with the organisations that define the emirate’s position as a leading international destination, DCT Abu Dhabi strives to unite the ecosystem around a shared vision of the emirate’s potential, coordinate effort and investment, deliver innovative solutions, and use the best tools, policies and systems to support the culture, creative and tourism industries.

DCT Abu Dhabi’s vision is defined by the emirate’s people, heritage and landscape. We work to enhance Abu Dhabi’s status as a place of authenticity, innovation, and unparalleled experiences, represented by its living traditions of hospitality, pioneering initiatives and creative thought.

For more information about the Department of Culture and Tourism – Abu Dhabi and the destination, please visit: dctabudhabi.ae and visitabudhabi.ae

Send us your press releases to pressrelease.zawya@lseg.com

data.title: Midcap sell-off deepens; Premier Energies, Waaree, ACME Solar, Aether, Laurus buck trend
data.description: Markets have seen sharp declines over the past month amid geopolitical tensions, with indices falling 9–13% and Bank Nifty down 16%. Market breadth has been extremely weak, with most stocks across Nifty, midcaps and smallcaps in the red. Despite the broad sell-off, select stocks in power and pharma have outperformed, supported by defensive demand and structural themes, with gains of up to 21% in some names.

data.title: Euro Could Rise if German Inflation Data Exceed Forecasts — Market Talk
data.description: The euro could rise at least temporarily if March German inflation data at 1200 GMT are higher than expected, Commerzbank's Thu Lan Nguyen says in a note. This would fuel expectations for a sharper acceleration in inflation due to rising energy prices resulting from the Middle East conflict. Potentially, this could lift already-pronounced expectations for interest-rate rises from the European Central Bank even higher, she says. The market expects the ECB will respond more quickly to the inflation shock than it did in 2022 when Russia's invasion of Ukraine pushed up energy prices. This has limited the euro's falls recently, she says. However, a sharper euro downturn is possible if the Iran war escalates, she says. The euro drops 0.1% to $1.1495.(renae.dyer@wsj.com)

data.title: Arion Bank: Transactions in relation to a share buyback programme
data.description:
Reference is made to a press release from Arion Bank, published 14 January 2026 regarding the launch of a share buyback program. In week 13 2026 Arion Bank purchased own shares on Nasdaq Iceland and Swedish Depository Receipts (SDR) on Nasdaq Stockholm. See further details below.

Share buyback on Nasdaq Iceland:

SDRs purchased on Nasdaq Stockholm:

DateTime (GMT)Number of SDRsShare pricePurchase price (SEK)Total own SDRs
23.3.202616:12:552,73313.2036,0763,677,620
24.3.202615:16:006213.308253,677,682

27.3.202609:57:011,97813.2526,2093,681,738
27.3.202609:57:013,02213.2540,0423,684,760
9,873130,7883,684,760

The Bank held 52,341,780 own shares and SDRs prior to the transactions in week 13 and holds 54,254,653 and SDRs by the end of week 13. The Bank currently holds 3.82% of issued shares in the Bank. Since the launch of the share buyback programme the Bank has bought in total 24,668,039 shares for ISK 4,829,637,065 and 161,328 SDRs for SEK 2,344,277.

The repurchase under the Program is divided between the Icelandic and Swedish markets, where up to 516,796 SDRs may be repurchased in Sweden, corresponding to 0.04% of the current issued capital, and where up to 25,322,997 shares may be repurchased in Iceland, corresponding to 1.78% of the current issued capital (total 1.82% of the current issued capital). The total consideration for purchased SDRs shall furthermore not exceed ISK 100,000,000 in Sweden and ISK 4,900,000,000 for purchased shares in Iceland (ISK 5.0bn total). The Program will end no later than 31 December 2026, subject to renewed authorisation for share buy-backs by the Bank´s AGM. The Bank retains the right to discontinue the Program at any time.

The Program is carried out in accordance with applicable law and regulation in Iceland and Sweden, as the case may be, including Regulation No. 596/2014 of the European Parliament and of the Council on market abuse (“MAR”), the Safe Harbour Regulation, Icelandic acts on limited liability companies, No. 2/1995 and Act No. 60 /2021 on measures against market manipulation and rules no. 1290/2025 of measures against market manipulation.

For any further information please contact:

Theodór Friðbertsson, Investor Relations at Arion Bank,ir@arionbanki.is, tel. +354 856 6760 or Eirikur Dor Jonsson, Head of Treasury at Arion Bank, eirikur.jonsson@arionbanki.is, tel. +354 856 7171

data.title: Origin Materials 10-K: Revenue $18.9M, EPS $(50.55) — large operating loss
data.description:
Origin Materials reported fiscal-year revenue of $18.922 million and a net loss per share of $(50.55), driven by a significant non-cash asset impairment and elevated operating expenses. The company posted a net loss of $(249.698) million and an operating loss of $(259.093) million, reflecting a major $195.636 million impairment charge. Origin Materials said revenue declined as a supply-chain activation program wound down, while operational changes are being implemented to extend runway and prioritize commercialization.

Financial Highlights

  • Total revenues: $18.922 million (decrease of $12.36 million, or 40%, vs prior year)
  • Gross profit: $0.541 million
  • Loss from operations: $(259.093) million (includes a $195.636 million impairment of assets)
  • Net loss: $(249.698) million
  • Net loss per share, basic and diluted: $(50.55) (weighted-average shares 4,939,958)

Business Highlights

  • Production scale-up: The first CapFormer line passed FAT in September 2024 and began production in Reed City in February 2025; additional lines are completing FAT/SAT with a goal of six lines by end of 2026.
  • Revenue and channel shift: Product revenue fell as the company wound down its supply-chain activation program; a pilot still-water launch in August 2025 is aimed at building commercial traction.
  • R&D focus: Research and development spending was reduced by 26% as furanics investment was paused and efforts narrowed to PET closures; further development is required for pressurized and carbonated formats.
  • Operational restructuring: A February 2026 realignment reduced workforce by approximately 32%, deferred non-beverage formats, and trimmed operating expenses to extend cash runway and prioritize closures commercialization.
  • Market strategy and partnerships: The company is pursuing a diversified manufacturing footprint, including a European partner, and conducting a strategic review to consider financings, licensing, or partnerships to accelerate adoption.

Original SEC Filing: Origin Materials, Inc. [ ORGN ] - 10-K - Mar. 27, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Jena Acquisition II 10-K: $0 Revenue, $1.84M Net Loss
data.description:
Jena Acquisition II reported no operating revenue for the period and recorded a net loss of $1.84 million, driven by general and administrative expenses while holding capital in trust for a potential business combination. Earnings per share were not presented in the filing. The company remains an early-stage blank-check entity preparing for a post-IPO business combination.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $0 (no operating revenues for the period)
  • Operating Income: $0 (company incurred operating expenses; no operating income)
  • Net Income: $1,836,775 net loss
  • Net Income Per Share: Not reported in the filing

Business Highlights

  • Business Focus: Targeting a post-IPO business combination; search prioritized by founders' historical industry expertise.
  • Operational Stage: Early-stage special purpose acquisition company (blank check) with no operating revenues; primary activities include IPO-related matters, target evaluation, and due diligence.
  • Capital Deployment: Approximately $230 million held in trust for an initial business combination; interest income from the trust is being used to fund limited pre-deal operations.
  • Public Company Readiness: Incurred increased public-company costs and advisory fees while building governance and transaction execution capabilities ahead of a potential merger.

Original SEC Filing: JENA ACQUISITION Corp II [ JENA ] - 10-K - Mar. 27, 2026

data.title: HyOrc 2025 10-K: Revenue $59K, EPS $(0.00)
data.description:
HyOrc reported net revenues of $59,124 for the year ended December 31, 2025, and a net loss of $551,294, which produced reported net loss per common share—basic and diluted—of $(0.00). The results reflect early-stage commercialization and limited revenue recognition during the period. Management highlights the need for financing, receivables collection and cost discipline to support ongoing operations.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $59,124 for the year ended Dec 31, 2025.
  • Operating Income: Loss from operations of $(565,812) for the year ended Dec 31, 2025.
  • Net Income: Net loss of $(551,294) for the year ended Dec 31, 2025.
  • Net Income Per Share: Net loss per common share—basic and diluted—reported as $(0.00).

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Mix and Commercialization: Revenue was driven primarily by engineering and technical services delivered through SRE Power, reflecting a channel shift toward services as part of early commercialization efforts.
  • Project Development: Entered a definitive agreement to develop a waste-to-methanol project in Bulgaria targeting processing of approximately 56,000 tonnes of RDF and producing an estimated 18,000–20,000 tonnes per year of methanol.
  • Intellectual Property and Deployment Strategy: Company strategy centers on monetizing a portfolio of patents and goodwill via licensing, engineering services and project development to accelerate technology deployment.
  • Operational Priorities: Management is prioritizing financing, collection of receivables and disciplined cost control to support scaling of operations and execution of development projects.

Original SEC Filing: HyOrc Corp [ HYOR ] - 10-K - Mar. 30, 2026

data.title: Gores Holdings X 2025 10-K: Net Income $0.92M, EPS $(0.76)
data.description:
Gores Holdings X reported a net income of $0.921 million for the year ended December 31, 2025, which includes a non-cash warrant fair value loss, and recorded a net loss from operations of $1.850 million. The company reported a basic and diluted net loss per ordinary share of $(0.76) for the period. There were no operating revenues as the company is a special purpose acquisition company focused on completing an initial business combination.

Financial Highlights

  • Net Income: $0.921 million for the year ended December 31, 2025 (includes non‑cash warrant fair value loss).
  • Net loss from operations / Operating Income: $(1.850) million for the year ended December 31, 2025.
  • Net loss per ordinary share / Net loss per common share: $(0.76) basic and diluted per share for the year ended December 31, 2025 (applies to Class A redeemable, Class A non‑redeemable, Class B shares).

Business Highlights

  • IPO and capital formation: Completed an initial public offering on May 5, 2025, raising approximately $358.8 million and a related $2.25 million private placement to support efforts to identify and complete a business combination.
  • Operational focus: The company had no operating revenue during the period; its primary activities are target identification, due diligence and negotiating an initial business combination.
  • Trust account deployment: Approximately $367.7 million held in a money market trust account designated to fund a business combination; interest from the trust may be used for limited operations.
  • Sponsor and advisory support: Sponsor provided initial funding and ongoing administrative services, with advisory and deferred fees structured around completion of a business combination.
  • Timeline and execution risk: Management expects to complete a business combination by May 5, 2027 and notes potential funding shortfalls if transaction costs exceed estimates.

Original SEC Filing: Gores Holdings X, Inc. / CI [ GTEN ] - 10-K - Mar. 27, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: CitroTech 2025 10-K: Revenue $2.38M, Net Loss $36.84M
data.description:
CitroTech reported fiscal 2025 revenue of $2.38 million, a 195% increase driven by product sales and installation services, while net loss widened to $36.84 million. Gross profit of $530,725 reflects margin pressure from higher inventory and rent, and operating loss was driven by $18.88 million of operating expenses. Net income per share was not disclosed in the filing.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $2.38 million, increased $1.57 million (195%) versus 2024, driven by product sales and installation services.
  • Gross Profit: $530,725 (reported as Revenue $2,381,407 less Cost of revenue $1,850,682); margin pressure noted from higher inventory and rent.
  • Operating Income: $(16.50) million, reflecting an operating loss driven by $18.88 million of total operating expenses (209% increase).
  • Net Income: $(36.84) million, net loss increased $29.96 million (435%) versus 2024 due to higher operating and other expenses.
  • Net Income Per Share: Not disclosed in Part 2 Item 7/Item 8 of the report.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Growth: Broader market adoption of CitroTech and CitroSafe systems contributed to the 195% year-over-year revenue increase.
  • Channel Shift: Transitioned from concentrated project sales to diversified channels including residential installations, fire departments, and municipal contracts.
  • Brand Momentum: Products earned EPA Safer Choice twice and UL GREENGUARD Gold certification and were adopted by California fire departments.
  • Operational Milestones: Launched CitroSafe proactive residential and commercial systems and initiated product installation services in 2024.

Original SEC Filing: CitroTech Inc. [ CITR ] - 10-K - Mar. 30, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: 21Shares Solana ETF 2025 10-K: NAV $5.735M, Net Loss $4.148M
data.description:
21Shares Solana ETF reported a net loss of $4.148 million for the period Sep 17–Dec 31, 2025, driven primarily by investment losses in SOL and related unrealized depreciation. The trust's Net Asset Value stood at $5.735 million as of Dec 31, 2025, reflecting growth in shares outstanding since launch. Sponsor fees and staking arrangements are noted as part of the fund's fee and revenue model.

Financial Highlights

  • Net Income: $-4.148 million — net decrease in net assets resulting from operations for the period Sep 17–Dec 31, 2025.
  • Net realized and change in unrealized loss on investment in SOL: $-4.324 million, which includes $-0.748 million of unrealized depreciation.
  • Net Asset Value (NAV): $5.735 million as of Dec 31, 2025 (used for daily valuation).

Business Highlights

  • Fundraising & Launch: Sponsor seeded the Trust with $100 for an initial 10,000 shares; the Trust launched and calculates NAV daily.
  • Asset Growth & Shares Issued: Shares outstanding increased to 460,000 by Dec 31, 2025, contributing to the NAV reaching $5.735 million.
  • Investment Objective & Operations: The Trust is a passive SOL ETF tracking the CF Benchmarks Pricing Benchmark, holding SOL in custody and with the ability to stake holdings.
  • Fee & Revenue Model: Sponsor charges a fee of 0.21% of NAV and retains 10% of staking rewards; Sponsor is responsible for routine operating expenses.
  • Valuation & Liquidity: NAV and Principal Market NAV are calculated daily; SOL is priced at 4:00pm ET and proceeds from SOL sales are used to pay sponsor fees.

Original SEC Filing: 21Shares Solana ETF [ TSOL ] - 10-K - Mar. 27, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Reviva Pharmaceuticals' 2025 net loss narrows on lower R&D spending
data.description:
Overview

  • US biopharmaceutical firm's fiscal 2025 net loss narrowed yr/yr

  • Fiscal 2025 EPS loss narrowed compared to prior yr

  • Company completed three equity offerings in 2025 and early 2026, boosting cash position

Outlook

  • Reviva plans to initiate RECOVER-2 Phase 3 trial for brilaroxazine in schizophrenia in mid-2026

  • Company expects trial-related activities for RECOVER-2 to start in Q2-2026, with U.S. enrollment in Q3-2026

  • Reviva believes current cash position will fund operations into Q1-2027

Result Drivers

  • R&D SPENDING - Lower research and development expenses contributed to a narrower net loss in 2025

  • CLINICAL PROGRESS - Company highlighted durable efficacy and safety data for brilaroxazine in schizophrenia, supporting ongoing regulatory efforts

Company press release:

Key Details

Beat/Miss

Actual

Consensus Estimate

FY EPS

-$5.48

-$19.90 mln

Analyst Coverage

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 5 "strong buy" or "buy", 1 "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell"

  • The average consensus recommendation for the biotechnology & medical research peer group is "buy"

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Reviva Pharmaceuticals Holdings Inc is $60.00, about 7,990.6% above its March 27 closing price of $0.74
